name: Five Lilies Chowder
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Mushroom stock or rich chicken stock (fat removed)
Kosher Salt & Freshly Ground Black Pepper
2 tsp Minced fresh thyme, or 1 tsp Dried thyme
2 tsp Minced fresh oregano, or 1 tsp Dried oregano
1 cup Leeks, both white and tender green parts
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
2. Add the diced onions, shallots, and garlic to the pan. Sauté until they begin to color slightly, about 5 minutes.
3. Transfer half of the onion mixture to a blender or food processor. Puree into a smooth paste and return it to the saucepan.
4. Stir in the white wine, bay leaf, and mushroom stock (or rich chicken stock with fat removed). Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat and cook for 10 minutes.
5. Season the soup base with salt and pepper. The soup can be prepared up to this point, stored refrigerated for up to 3 days, or frozen indefinitely and reheated before finishing.
6. Just before serving, add the thyme (fresh or dried), oregano (fresh or dried), celery, leeks, and sherry to the hot soup base in a saucepan.
7. Bring the mixture back to a simmer over medium heat and cook for 2 minutes. Do not overcook; the vegetables should retain their crunchy texture.
8. Remove the bay leaf from the pot, stir in the chives, and garnish with a sprinkling of gremolata before serving.
Yield: 8 servings
